title: mentha gentilis var. grata
intro: Mentha gentilis var. grata, also known as Corn Mint, is a subspecies of the wild mint plant that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Corn Mint include relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and indigestion, as well as its ability to alleviate headaches and soothe sore throats. Corn Mint can be prepared as a herbal tea, oil, or infused in water to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
